evidence: QueryExecutor interface defines: findCollocations(lemma, cqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults), executeSurfacePattern(lemma, bcqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults), executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults) — consistent 4-arg signature with minLogDice.

executeBcqlQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults) breaks the pattern: no lemma, no minLogDice. Callers who want logDice filtering must post-filter results manually, while all other query methods filter at query time.

executeQuery(cqlPattern, maxResults) also has no minLogDice param and returns ConcordanceResult (not WordSketchResult), which is a different result type than all other methods — the interface mixes two result shapes.

The deprecated 3-arg executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) is still part of the public interface. The @Deprecated annotation and Javadoc are clear but the method remains in the interface and must be implemented by test stubs.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore requires a query parameter named 'seeds' (plural) even though it accepts only a single seed and stores it in a variable named 'seed'. The URL schema is: ?seeds=house (singular value, plural name).

REST URL inconsistency: sketch endpoints use path parameters (/api/sketch/{lemma}) while exploration endpoints use query parameters (?seed=house). The mixing of conventions is documented but not enforced by any abstraction.

HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams uses String.split('=', 2) — correct — but does not URL-decode keys, only values. A percent-encoded parameter name would silently fail to match. (Minor but an API boundary issue.)
WordSketchApiServer binds only to loopback without documentation at the API boundary — callers attempting to expose the API on a network interface will get a connection-refused without a clear error.

strengths: The main query methods (findCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, executeDependencyPattern, executeDependencyPatternWithPos) share a consistent 4-argument signature with uniform parameter ordering.
RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias provides a single canonical location for alias resolution, preventing duplicated alias maps across handlers.
ExploreOptions bundles the 5 exploration tuning parameters instead of spreading them across method arguments.
The @Deprecated annotation and migration Javadoc on the legacy executeDependencyPattern overload are clear and actionable.
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: The interface mixes two result shapes (WordSketchResult vs ConcordanceResult/CollocateResult) and has one method (executeBcqlQuery) with a structurally different signature from its siblings, creating a non-uniform API surface that callers must understand in detail.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The primary query methods are internally consistent, which is a genuine strength. However, executeBcqlQuery's signature asymmetry, the plural 'seeds' parameter for a single-seed endpoint, and the mixing of path vs query parameter conventions across sibling endpoints are confirmed design inconsistencies that complicate client usage. The score of 64 reflects a mostly coherent but visibly patchy surface.

issues.summary: executeBcqlQuery lacks a minLogDice parameter unlike all sibling query methods
evidence: findCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, executeDependencyPattern all accept (lemma, ..., double minLogDice, int maxResults).
executeBcqlQuery(String bcqlPattern, int maxResults) has no minLogDice — callers cannot request score-filtered BCQL results; they must post-filter.
CollocateQueryHelper.scoreHits computes logDice per hit but there is no minLogDice guard in the pipeline.
issues.suggestion: Add a minLogDice parameter to executeBcqlQuery (defaulting to 0.0 via an overload or default method), and pass it through to scoreHits in CollocateQueryHelper to filter before returning.

issues.summary: Single-seed explore endpoint requires a query parameter named 'seeds' (plural) but accepts only one value
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore calls HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, "seeds") and assigns the result to String seed.
The API documentation comment says: ?seed=house&relation=adj_predicate but the code requires 'seeds' (plural).
Clients using the documented 'seed' parameter name will receive a 400 error.
issues.suggestion: Rename the parameter to 'seed' (singular) in handleSemanticFieldExplore to match the documented single-seed semantics; update the Javadoc comment accordingly. Keep 'seeds' only for the multi-seed endpoint.

issues.summary: The deprecated 5-argument executeDependencyPattern overload remains in the public interface, requiring implementation by all stubs
evidence: QueryExecutor declares a @Deprecated default method executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) that delegates to the non-deprecated variants.

SemanticFieldExplorerTest.StubExecutor implements executeDependencyPattern (2-arg) and executeDependencyPatternWithPos but also inherits the deprecated default — test stubs are coupled to a method that has no callers.
No production code calls the 5-arg form per codebase search.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 5-arg deprecated overload from the QueryExecutor interface; add a minor-version note to CLAUDE.md or a CHANGELOG entry explaining the removal. If backward compatibility with external callers is needed, retain only as a static utility method outside the interface.

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams URL-decodes values but not keys, silently dropping percent-encoded parameter names
evidence: parseQueryParams splits on '&' and '=', then calls URLDecoder.decode on keyValue[1] (value) and keyValue[0] (key).
Wait — re-reading the code: params.put(URLDecoder.decode(keyValue[0], 'UTF-8'), URLDecoder.decode(keyValue[1], 'UTF-8')). Both key and value are decoded. This is correct.
False positive: both key and value are decoded in the same try block.
issues.suggestion: No action needed — this was a false positive upon closer inspection. Both keys and values are URL-decoded correctly.

root_causes: executeBcqlQuery was added as a lower-level escape hatch before the main query methods were standardised, so it never received the minLogDice parameter.
The 'seeds' parameter name in the single-seed endpoint is likely a copy-paste from the multi-seed endpoint during refactoring.
